heading: Activities to support safe and healthy students

children.text: are coordinated with other schools and community-based services and programs;
foster safe, healthy, supportive, and drug-free environments that support student academic achievement;
promote the involvement of parents in the activity or program;

may be conducted in partnership with an institution of higher education, business, nonprofit organization, community-based organization, or other public or private entity with a demonstrated record of success in implementing activities described in this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: programs to educate students against the use of alcohol, tobacco, marijuana, smokeless tobacco products, and electronic cigarettes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: professional development and training for school and specialized instructional support personnel and interested community members in prevention, education, early identification, intervention mentoring, recovery support services and, where appropriate, rehabilitation referral, as related to drug and violence prevention;

children.label: i
children.text: school-based mental health services, including early identification of mental health symptoms, drug use, and violence, and appropriate referrals to direct individual or group counseling services, which may be provided by school-based mental health services providers; and

children.label: I
children.text: are conducted in partnership with a public or private mental health entity or health care entity; and

children.label: aa
children.text: based on trauma-informed practices that are evidence-based (to the extent the State, in consultation with local educational agencies in the State, determines that such evidence is reasonably available);

children.label: bb
children.text: coordinated (where appropriate) with early intervening services provided under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.); and20 U.S.C. 1400

children.label: cc
children.text: provided by qualified mental and behavioral health professionals who are certified or licensed by the State involved and practicing within their area of expertise;

children.label: i
children.text: integrate health and safety practices into school or athletic programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: support a healthy, active lifestyle, including nutritional education and regular, structured physical education activities and programs, that may address chronic disease management with instruction led by school nurses, nurse practitioners, or other appropriate specialists or professionals to help maintain the well-being of students;

children.label: iii
children.text: help prevent bullying and harassment;

children.label: iv
children.text: improve instructional practices for developing relationship-building skills, such as effective communication, and improve safety through the recognition and prevention of coercion, violence, or abuse, including teen and dating violence, stalking, domestic abuse, and sexual violence and harassment;

children.label: v
children.text: provide mentoring and school counseling to all students, including children who are at risk of academic failure, dropping out of school, involvement in criminal or delinquent activities, or drug use and abuse;

children.label: vi
children.text: establish or improve school dropout and re-entry programs; or

children.label: vii
children.text: establish learning environments and enhance students’ effective learning skills that are essential for school readiness and academic success, such as by providing integrated systems of student and family supports;

children.label: i
children.text: suicide prevention;

children.label: ii
children.text: effective and trauma-informed practices in classroom management;

children.label: iii
children.text: crisis management and conflict resolution techniques;

children.label: iv
children.text: human trafficking (defined, for purposes of this subparagraph, as an act or practice described in paragraph (9) or (10) of );1section 7102 of title 22See References in Text note below.1

children.label: v
children.text: school-based violence prevention strategies;

children.label: vi
children.text: drug abuse prevention, including educating children facing substance abuse at home; and

children.label: vii
children.text: bullying and harassment prevention;

children.label: i
children.text: age-appropriate and developmentally-appropriate instruction for students in child sexual abuse awareness and prevention, including how to recognize child sexual abuse and how to safely report child sexual abuse; and

children.label: ii
children.text: information to parents and guardians of students about child sexual abuse awareness and prevention, including how to recognize child sexual abuse and how to discuss child sexual abuse with a child;

children.label: i
children.text: is consistent with best practices;

children.label: ii
children.text: includes strategies that are evidence-based (to the extent the State, in consultation with local educational agencies in the State, determines that such evidence is reasonably available); and

children.label: iii
children.text: is aligned with the long-term goal of prison reduction through opportunities, mentoring, intervention, support, and other education services, referred to as a “youth PROMISE plan”; or

children.label: G
children.text: implementation of schoolwide positive behavioral interventions and supports, including through coordination with similar activities carried out under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.), in order to improve academic outcomes and school conditions for student learning;20 U.S.C. 1400

children.label: i
children.text: establishing partnerships within the community to provide resources and support for schools;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensuring that all service and community partners are aligned with the academic expectations of a community school in order to improve student success; and

children.label: iii
children.text: strengthening relationships between schools and communities; or

children.label: I
children.text: pay for success initiatives aligned with the purposes of this section.
